note: Patient-factor modifier engine: rules keyed to intake/lab/medication/comorbidity/symptom/procedure fields that modify risk classification for a candidate peptide pathway. Scope: lock-all / fire-formulary — each rule carries fires_in_formulary; rules whose agent groups have no stocked member (amylin, exenatide, desmopressin/ADH, PTH) are retained as informational and never fire. Single-visit threshold logic only (no longitudinal/trend firing). No doses, no prescribing, no regulatory status. Decision-support; the provider decides.

grades.B: human pathway/signal evidence, not exact combo/subgroup

rules.name: Missing renal function before renal/water-clearance-sensitive pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Do not classify renal-risk pathways as low risk when eGFR/creatinine are missing; request renal function data or hold final risk classification for clinician review.
rules.rationale: Renal thresholds appear in exenatide, desmopressin, teriparatide, and related labeling; absent renal data prevents accurate rule firing.
rules.evidence_directness: operational gate supported by label-based renal rules

rules.name: Missing serum sodium before vasopressin/desmopressin-axis pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Do not classify desmopressin/ADH-axis agents as low risk when sodium is missing; request sodium or clinician review.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin labeling centers on hyponatremia risk and sodium monitoring.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label supported

rules.name: Missing diabetes control metrics before incretin/amylin or GH-axis pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Request A1c and/or glucose baseline before final glycemic-risk classification.
rules.rationale: Incretin/amylin agents and GH-axis agents can intersect with glycemic control, hypoglycemia risk, or glucose intolerance.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for labels; pathway extrapolation for research agents

rules.name: Missing medication list before medication-interaction-sensitive pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Do not clear interaction-sensitive pathways when medication list is absent; request current medications, including OTC NSAIDs and supplements.
rules.rationale: Several label-supported rules depend on concomitant insulin/SU, hyponatremia-risk drugs, naltrexone, oral contraceptives, digoxin, and oral drug absorption.
rules.evidence_directness: operational gate supported by label-based interaction rules

rules.name: Missing GI history before delayed-gastric-emptying pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Ask for gastroparesis/severe GI disease history before classifying delayed-gastric-emptying pathway risk.
rules.rationale: Pramlintide and tirzepatide labels specifically address gastroparesis/severe GI disease; GLP-1 and melanocortin labeling include gastric emptying/oral absorption language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for pramlintide/tirzepatide/bremelanotide; class/pathway inference for investigational agents

rules.name: Missing endocrine tumor/family history before incretin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Request MTC/MEN2 history before final risk classification for GLP-1/GIP incretin pathways.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide and tirzepatide labels include MTC/MEN2 contraindication language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for semaglutide/tirzepatide; class/pathway inference for retatrutide

rules.name: Missing malignancy history before GH/IGF or regenerative/angiogenic pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Do not provide low-risk wording for GH/IGF or regenerative/immune-modulating pathways without malignancy history.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin/somatropin labels support strong malignancy-context rules; regenerative/angiogenic peptide claims are lower-confidence and should not be made without oncology context.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin/somatropin; low-confidence mechanistic for regenerative research peptides

rules.name: Missing pregnancy/lactation status before reproductive, metabolic, GH, ADH, PTH, or research pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Request pregnancy/lactation status before final risk classification; do not infer absence.
rules.rationale: Multiple labels include pregnancy-related risk language; for investigational/research peptides, human pregnancy/lactation data are commonly insufficient.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for labeled agents; evidence-gap rule for research peptides

rules.name: Missing upcoming anesthesia/deep-sedation procedure status before incretin/amylin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Ask about upcoming general anesthesia/deep sedation before final delayed-gastric-emptying risk classification.
rules.rationale: Tirzepatide labeling includes pulmonary aspiration reports during general anesthesia or deep sedation and delayed gastric emptying language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tirzepatide; pathway inference for GLP-1/amylin/polyagonist agents

rules.name: Missing calcium/bone-risk history before PTH/PTHrP pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Request calcium and bone-risk history before final PTH/PTHrP pathway risk classification.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling supports specific calcium, urolithiasis, skeletal malignancy/radiation, Paget, and osteosarcoma-risk screening concepts.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Insulin or insulin secretagogue with GLP-1/GIP/amylin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Flag increased hypoglycemia risk; do not classify as low risk without clinician review and a glucose-monitoring concept.
rules.rationale: GLP-1/GIP and amylin pathways have low intrinsic hypoglycemia risk in some contexts, but labels support increased hypoglycemia risk when combined with insulin or insulin secretagogues.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for labeled semaglutide/tirzepatide/pramlintide; pathway inference for retatrutide/cagrilintide

rules.name: Pramlintide plus insulin severe hypoglycemia gate
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate pramlintide-insulin combinations for clinician review; never present as routine low-risk logic.
rules.rationale: Pramlintide labeling contains boxed warning language for severe hypoglycemia with insulin.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Hypoglycemia unawareness with amylin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate; do not issue nonchalant or low-risk language for amylin-pathway agents when hypoglycemia unawareness is present.
rules.rationale: Pramlintide labeling lists hypoglycemia unawareness as a contraindication; extension to non-pramlintide amylin analogues is a conservative pathway inference.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for pramlintide; extrapolated for cagrilintide

rules.name: Recurrent severe hypoglycemia history with amylin/incretin pathway and glucose-lowering meds
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate for clinician review; patient history suggests higher consequence if glycemic therapy is intensified or gastric emptying changes.
rules.rationale: Pramlintide labeling excludes recurrent severe hypoglycemia; incretin labels support hypoglycemia risk with insulin/secretagogues.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for pramlintide and GLP-1/GIP labels; class inference for cagrilintide

rules.name: High HbA1c context with pramlintide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag as label-based patient-selection concern for clinician review.
rules.rationale: Pramlintide labeling includes patient-selection language involving HbA1c above 9%.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Gastroparesis with pramlintide or amylin analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate; do not present amylin pathway as low-risk in confirmed gastroparesis.
rules.rationale: Pramlintide labeling contraindicates confirmed gastroparesis; cagrilintide extension is pathway-based rather than direct.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for pramlintide; extrapolated for cagrilintide

rules.name: Severe gastroparesis or severe GI disease with tirzepatide/GLP-1 pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate; delayed-gastric-emptying pathway can be clinically relevant in severe GI disease.
rules.rationale: Tirzepatide labeling states it is not recommended in severe gastroparesis and includes severe GI disease warnings; semaglutide label supports delayed gastric emptying language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tirzepatide; class/pathway inference for semaglutide/retatrutide

rules.name: Oral medication absorption caution with delayed-gastric-emptying agents
rules.provider_behavior: Flag possible altered oral medication absorption/timing; do not make a blanket no-interaction claim.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide, tirzepatide, pramlintide, and bremelanotide labeling include delayed gastric emptying or oral medication absorption warnings.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for listed labeled agents; pathway inference for analogues

rules.name: Oral hormonal contraceptive with tirzepatide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag label-specific oral contraceptive interaction; do not provide final clearance without clinician review.
rules.rationale: Tirzepatide labeling states delayed gastric emptying may reduce oral hormonal contraceptive exposure.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Upcoming anesthesia/deep sedation with GLP-1/GIP/amylin delayed-gastric-emptying pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Flag peri-procedural review; do not generate simplistic stop/continue instructions.
rules.rationale: Tirzepatide labeling includes pulmonary aspiration reports during general anesthesia/deep sedation in patients receiving GLP-1 receptor agonists and delayed gastric emptying context.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tirzepatide label; pathway inference for delayed-gastric-emptying agents

rules.name: Severe GI symptoms with incretin/amylin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate severe/persistent abdominal pain or dehydrating GI symptoms; do not provide reassurance-only output.
rules.rationale: GLP-1/GIP labels include pancreatitis, gallbladder disease, severe GI adverse reactions, and acute kidney injury due volume depletion warnings.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for semaglutide/tirzepatide; pathway inference for retatrutide/amylin combinations

rules.name: Volume depletion or AKI risk with GLP-1/GIP pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate dehydration/AKI context; do not present GI adverse effects as cosmetic or trivial.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide, tirzepatide, and exenatide labels contain renal injury warnings related to GI fluid losses/dehydration.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for semaglutide/tirzepatide/exenatide; inference for retatrutide

rules.name: History of pancreatitis with incretin/amylin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Flag history of pancreatitis for clinician review; do not claim absence of risk.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide and tirzepatide labels include pancreatitis warnings; direct data for every analogue/combination are incomplete.
rules.evidence_directness: direct warning for labeled agents; pathway inference for investigational agents

rules.name: Gallbladder disease or biliary symptoms with GLP-1/GIP/amylin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Flag gallbladder/biliary history or symptoms; do not classify as no known issue.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide and tirzepatide labels include acute gallbladder disease warnings.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for semaglutide/tirzepatide; class/pathway inference for combinations

rules.name: Diabetic retinopathy with rapid glycemic-improvement pathways
rules.provider_behavior: Flag retinopathy context; do not ignore baseline eye disease when glycemic or GH/IGF axis may shift.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide and tirzepatide labels include retinopathy complication warnings; tesamorelin/somatropin labels also address diabetes/retinopathy context in GH-axis treatment.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported for named agents; pathway inference for GH secretagogues

rules.name: MTC/MEN2 history with GLP-1/GIP incretin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate for clinician review; do not provide low-risk wording for GLP-1/GIP pathway in MTC/MEN2 history.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide and tirzepatide labeling includes contraindication language for personal/family MTC or MEN2.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for semaglutide/tirzepatide; pathway inference for retatrutide

rules.name: Active suicidal ideation or serious psychiatric instability with weight-management incretin pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate active suicidality/recent attempt; do not generate weight-loss optimization recommendations without clinician review.
rules.rationale: Semaglutide weight-management labeling contains suicidal behavior/ideation monitoring and avoidance language in patients with active ideation or history of attempt.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for semaglutide weight-management labeling; not direct for all incretin agents

rules.name: Resting tachycardia/palpitations with retatrutide or glucagon-containing polyagonist
rules.provider_behavior: Flag heart-rate/palpitations context for retatrutide; do not claim cardiac neutrality.
rules.rationale: Human retatrutide trial reporting includes heart-rate increase signals.
rules.evidence_directness: direct human trial signal but not label-based broad rule

rules.name: CrCl/eGFR below 30 with exenatide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag label-based severe renal impairment concern for exenatide.
rules.rationale: Exenatide labeling states it is not recommended in severe renal impairment or ESRD.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Moderate renal impairment with exenatide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag moderate renal impairment context for exenatide.
rules.rationale: Exenatide labeling urges caution in moderate renal impairment and notes renal injury reports.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Renal transplant with exenatide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag renal transplant context for exenatide; do not present as standard low-risk.
rules.rationale: Exenatide labeling notes not studied in renal transplantation.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: eGFR below 50 with desmopressin/ADH-axis agent
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate eGFR below 50 for desmopressin/ADH-axis pathway.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin labeling includes renal impairment/eGFR below 50 as contraindication context due hyponatremia/water retention risk.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for desmopressin; pathway inference for ADH-axis agents

rules.name: Hyponatremia or history of hyponatremia with desmopressin
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate; do not output desmopressin/ADH-axis clearance in hyponatremia or history of hyponatremia.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin labeling contraindicates use in hyponatremia or history of hyponatremia.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Loop diuretic or systemic/inhaled glucocorticoid with desmopressin
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate label-supported interaction risk; do not treat as routine monitoring-only.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin nocturia labeling lists loop diuretics and systemic/inhaled glucocorticoids as contraindicated concomitant therapies because of hyponatremia risk.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Hyponatremia-risk medications with desmopressin
rules.provider_behavior: Flag additive hyponatremia risk and sodium-monitoring need; do not issue low-risk language.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin labeling identifies several drug classes that can increase hyponatremia risk and recommends more frequent sodium monitoring.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Age 65 or older with desmopressin
rules.provider_behavior: Flag higher sodium-monitoring risk context in older adults.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin nocturia labeling states more frequent sodium monitoring is recommended for patients aged 65 or older and those at risk for hyponatremia.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Heart failure or uncontrolled hypertension with desmopressin
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate heart failure/uncontrolled hypertension for desmopressin/water-retention pathway.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin nocturia labeling contraindicates heart failure or uncontrolled hypertension because fluid retention may worsen these conditions.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Polydipsia/SIADH/fluid-electrolyte illness with desmopressin
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate desmopressin/ADH-axis logic when conditions predisposing to fluid/electrolyte imbalance are present.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin labeling includes polydipsia, SIADH, and illnesses causing fluid/electrolyte imbalance among contraindication/risk contexts.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Raised intracranial pressure risk or urinary retention history with desmopressin
rules.provider_behavior: Flag raised intracranial pressure or urinary retention history for clinician review.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin nocturia labeling says it is not recommended in patients at risk for increased intracranial pressure or with history of urinary retention.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Active malignancy with GH/IGF-1 axis
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate active malignancy; do not present GH/IGF-1-axis agents as low-risk or anti-aging routine.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin and somatropin labeling contain active malignancy/neoplasm risk language; application to secretagogues is a pathway inference.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin/somatropin; indirect for GHRH/GHS research peptides

rules.name: Prior malignancy or current surveillance with GH/IGF-1 axis
rules.provider_behavior: Require documented oncology-context review; do not describe as routine wellness synergy.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin labeling states preexisting malignancy should be carefully evaluated and treatment discontinued with recurrent malignancy; somatropin labeling includes neoplasm monitoring language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin/somatropin; indirect for secretagogues

rules.name: Elevated IGF-1 with GH/IGF-1 axis
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate elevated/persistently elevated IGF-1; do not add GH-axis load without clinician review.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin labeling includes IGF-1 monitoring and persistent elevation language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin; pathway inference for GH secretagogues and GHRH analogues

rules.name: Diabetes, prediabetes, or elevated A1c with GH/IGF-1 axis
rules.provider_behavior: Flag baseline diabetes/prediabetes for GH-axis agents; do not assume glucose neutrality.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin labeling includes glucose intolerance/diabetes risk and HbA1c monitoring concepts.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin; pathway inference for other GH-axis agents

rules.name: Active proliferative or severe non-proliferative diabetic retinopathy with GH-axis
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate active/severe diabetic retinopathy; do not issue routine GH-axis clearance.
rules.rationale: Somatropin labeling includes active proliferative or severe non-proliferative diabetic retinopathy contraindication; tesamorelin labeling includes diabetic retinopathy monitoring.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for somatropin; direct monitoring warning for tesamorelin; extrapolated for secretagogues

rules.name: Edema, heart failure, carpal tunnel, or fluid retention history with GH/IGF pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Flag fluid-retention-prone history before GH-axis agents.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin labeling includes fluid retention warnings; somatropin class labeling also supports fluid-retention concern.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin/somatropin; indirect for secretagogues

rules.name: Acute critical illness, post-operative complication, trauma, or respiratory failure with GH-axis
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate acute critical illness/post-op/respiratory failure context; no wellness optimization language.
rules.rationale: Somatropin labeling contraindicates use in acute critical illness due complications following open heart/abdominal surgery, multiple accidental trauma, or acute respiratory failure; tesamorelin label also contains acute critical illness language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for somatropin/tesamorelin; pathway inference for secretagogues

rules.name: Hypothalamic-pituitary disruption with tesamorelin/GHRH-axis
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate hypothalamic-pituitary axis disruption; do not assume secretagogue response or safety.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin labeling includes contraindication around disruption of the hypothalamic-pituitary axis due tumor/surgery/hypopituitarism/head irradiation/head trauma.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin; inference for CJC-1295/sermorelin

rules.name: Pregnancy with tesamorelin/GH-axis or research secretagogue
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate pregnancy/possible pregnancy; do not generate routine GH-axis recommendations.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin labeling lists pregnancy as a contraindication context for its indicated use; research secretagogue pregnancy data are insufficient.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin; evidence gap for research secretagogues

rules.name: Glucocorticoid replacement or suspected hypoadrenalism with GH/GHRH pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Flag adrenal/glucocorticoid replacement context; endocrine review before final classification.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin labeling includes language about possible interactions in patients treated with glucocorticoid replacement for hypoadrenalism.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for tesamorelin; indirect for other GH/GHRH agents

rules.name: Hypercalcemia or hypercalcemic disorder with PTH/PTHrP analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate hypercalcemia context; do not classify PTH/PTHrP pathway as low risk.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling advises avoidance in pre-existing hypercalcemia or hypercalcemic disorders.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for abaloparatide/PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Osteosarcoma-risk conditions with PTH/PTHrP analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate osteosarcoma-risk conditions; no routine clearance.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling advises avoiding use in patients with increased baseline osteosarcoma risk, including Paget disease, bone metastases/skeletal malignancy, skeletal radiation, open epiphyses, or hereditary osteosarcoma predisposition.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Urolithiasis or hypercalciuria with PTH/PTHrP analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Flag stone/hypercalciuria context.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling warns around active or recent urolithiasis and hypercalciuria.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Digoxin with PTH/PTHrP analogue or hypercalcemia
rules.provider_behavior: Flag digoxin; avoid no-interaction language.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling cautions that hypercalcemia may predispose to digitalis toxicity and advises caution with digoxin.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Calciphylaxis-risk context with PTH/PTHrP analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Flag calciphylaxis/cutaneous calcification risk context.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling notes calciphylaxis/cutaneous calcification has been reported and risk factors include autoimmune disease, kidney failure, and concomitant warfarin or systemic corticosteroids.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Orthostatic hypotension/falls/syncope history with PTH/PTHrP analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Flag orthostatic/fall-risk context.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling describes transient orthostatic hypotension.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Severe renal impairment with teriparatide/PTH analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Flag severe renal impairment; consider CKD-mineral bone context.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling notes altered pharmacokinetics in severe renal impairment.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Pregnancy or lactation with PTH/PTHrP analogue
rules.provider_behavior: Flag pregnancy/lactation context for clinician review.
rules.rationale: Teriparatide labeling includes pregnancy and lactation sections; production CDS should not assume safety in pregnancy/lactation.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for teriparatide; class inference for PTHrP analogues

rules.name: Uncontrolled hypertension or known cardiovascular disease with bremelanotide/PT-141
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate uncontrolled hypertension or known CVD; do not treat PT-141 as a cosmetic low-risk add-on.
rules.rationale: Bremelanotide labeling contraindicates use in uncontrolled hypertension or known cardiovascular disease and describes BP/HR effects.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported for bremelanotide/PT-141

rules.name: High cardiovascular risk or multiple uncontrolled risk factors with bremelanotide/PT-141
rules.provider_behavior: Flag cardiovascular risk context; do not describe as free of hemodynamic effect.
rules.rationale: Bremelanotide labeling warns about transient BP increases and HR decreases; cardiovascular context requires clinician interpretation.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported signal; patient risk stratification is CDS inference

rules.name: Oral naltrexone-containing product with bremelanotide/PT-141
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate oral naltrexone interaction; do not classify as no known issue.
rules.rationale: Bremelanotide labeling says it may significantly decrease systemic exposure of orally administered naltrexone and recommends avoiding use with oral naltrexone-containing products intended to treat alcohol or opioid addiction.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Threshold-dependent oral medication with bremelanotide/PT-141
rules.provider_behavior: Flag oral medication absorption/timing issue.
rules.rationale: Bremelanotide labeling states it may slow gastric emptying and potentially reduce rate/extent of absorption of oral medications.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Pregnancy or possible pregnancy with bremelanotide/PT-141
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate pregnancy/possible pregnancy; no routine PT-141 recommendation.
rules.rationale: Bremelanotide labeling contains pregnancy-risk language.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Hyperpigmentation risk context with bremelanotide/PT-141
rules.provider_behavior: Flag hyperpigmentation counseling/monitoring concept; do not overstate reversibility.
rules.rationale: Bremelanotide labeling includes focal hyperpigmentation warning.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported

rules.name: Fertility therapy or gonadotropin/GnRH-active medication with kisspeptin/GnRH pathway
rules.provider_behavior: Flag HPG-axis overlap; do not claim synergy or safety without reproductive endocrinology context.
rules.rationale: Kisspeptin stimulates GnRH/LH pathways; direct combination evidence with fertility regimens is context-dependent.
rules.evidence_directness: human/mechanistic endocrine evidence; not direct safety rule for all combinations

rules.name: Hormone-sensitive malignancy history with HPG-axis stimulant
rules.provider_behavior: Flag hormone-sensitive cancer history; classify interaction as theoretical unless direct agent-specific evidence exists.
rules.rationale: Kisspeptin/GnRH-axis agents stimulate reproductive neuroendocrine signaling; oncology implications require disease-specific clinician interpretation.
rules.evidence_directness: mechanistic inference

rules.name: Pregnancy or possible pregnancy with HPG-axis investigational peptide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag pregnancy context; do not generate fertility/hormonal optimization output without clinician review.
rules.rationale: Kisspeptin is a reproductive-axis peptide; pregnancy-specific use and combination safety are not established generically.
rules.evidence_directness: mechanistic/evidence-gap

rules.name: Autoimmune disease or biologic immunomodulator with immune-modulating research peptide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag immune-modulation uncertainty; use cautious wording and avoid direct safety/synergy claims.
rules.rationale: Thymosin alpha-1, LL-37, GHK-Cu, BPC-157 and related peptides have immune/regenerative pathway evidence, but direct patient-factor interaction data are limited.
rules.evidence_directness: mechanistic/indirect; evidence gap for most clinical combinations

rules.name: Transplant or major immunosuppression with immune-modulating research peptide
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate transplant/immunosuppression context; do not make immune-boosting claims.
rules.rationale: Immune-modulating peptide literature is insufficient to support broad safety claims in transplant/immunosuppressed patients.
rules.evidence_directness: mechanistic/evidence gap

rules.name: Active malignancy/history with regenerative or angiogenic research peptides
rules.provider_behavior: Flag oncology context; use low-confidence language and do not claim cancer safety.
rules.rationale: GHK-Cu and LL-37 literature involves tissue remodeling/angiogenesis/wound biology; BPC-157 evidence is largely preclinical/translational. This supports caution, not a proven cancer interaction.
rules.evidence_directness: mechanistic/indirect/evidence gap

rules.name: Active infection with immune/wound peptide marketed as anti-infective or immune support
rules.provider_behavior: Do not allow the AI to substitute peptide logic for standard infection evaluation/treatment.
rules.rationale: LL-37 and thymosin alpha-1 have immune/antimicrobial pathway literature, but this does not establish that research peptides replace standard infectious disease care.
rules.evidence_directness: mechanistic/indirect

rules.name: Pregnancy/lactation with research peptides lacking direct human data
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate; absence of direct data must not be converted into reassurance.
rules.rationale: For many research peptides, direct human pregnancy/lactation safety data are insufficient; mechanism-only evidence is not adequate for a no-risk claim.
rules.evidence_directness: evidence gap

rules.name: Research peptide with no direct human combination data
rules.provider_behavior: Force the AI agent to say 'theoretical/mechanistic' and not 'proven safe' or 'proven synergistic'.
rules.rationale: Mechanistic and preclinical literature cannot establish direct clinical combination safety.
rules.evidence_directness: evidence gap / methodological guardrail

rules.name: MOTS-c or metabolic research peptide with diabetes/glucose medications
rules.provider_behavior: Use theoretical language; do not claim direct glucose-safety or fat-loss synergy.
rules.rationale: MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived peptide involved in metabolic biology, but review literature notes clinical translation remains uncertain.
rules.evidence_directness: mechanistic/evidence gap

rules.name: Mitochondrial disease/cardiomyopathy context with SS-31/elamipretide
rules.provider_behavior: Flag specialist-disease context; do not extrapolate trial findings to unsupervised broad use.
rules.rationale: Elamipretide/SS-31 has human trial literature in selected mitochondrial disease contexts, but those data do not justify broad patient-factor claims.
rules.evidence_directness: direct human trial context but limited generalizability

rules.name: Neuropsychiatric instability or seizure disorder with neuropeptide research agents
rules.provider_behavior: Use uncertainty language and require clinician review; do not claim no neuropsychiatric interaction.
rules.rationale: For Semax/Selank/DSIP/Cerebrolysin-type neuropeptide research agents, accessible evidence does not establish robust patient-factor interaction rules for psychiatric instability, seizure disorder, or combinations; conservative clinician review is a language-safety rule.
rules.evidence_directness: evidence gap

rules.name: High cumulative GI-motility load
rules.provider_behavior: Flag combined GI-motility burden; avoid stacking-as-synergy language.
rules.rationale: Multiple cited labels support delayed gastric emptying/oral absorption or GI adverse-event signals for GLP-1/GIP, amylin, and bremelanotide pathways.
rules.evidence_directness: class/polypathway inference from direct labels

rules.name: High cumulative glycemic-lowering load
rules.provider_behavior: Flag combined glycemic-lowering burden with insulin/secretagogues.
rules.rationale: Labels support hypoglycemia risk when incretin/amylin agents interact with insulin or secretagogues.
rules.evidence_directness: direct label-supported classes; cumulative load is CDS inference

rules.name: High cumulative GH/IGF-1 pathway load
rules.provider_behavior: Flag stacked GH/IGF-axis logic; avoid claiming clean synergy unless direct evidence supports the exact combination.
rules.rationale: Tesamorelin and somatropin labels support IGF-1, glucose, fluid retention, malignancy and retinopathy concern domains.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for labels; cumulative stack is pathway inference

rules.name: High cumulative sodium/water-retention load
rules.provider_behavior: Escalate cumulative water/sodium risk; no low-risk label when any major sodium risk factor exists.
rules.rationale: Desmopressin labeling supports hyponatremia/water-retention risk factors and interacting medication rules.
rules.evidence_directness: direct for desmopressin; pathway inference for ADH-related agents

rules.name: No direct combination evidence means no direct safety/synergy claim
rules.provider_behavior: Use calibrated terms: theoretical, mechanistic, indirect, uncertain. Do not claim proven safety, proven synergy, or proven harm.
rules.rationale: Direct human evidence is not interchangeable with preclinical or mechanism-only literature.
rules.evidence_directness: methodological guardrail

rules.name: Red-rule override requires clinician review
rules.provider_behavior: If any red rule fires, the AI must not produce an a low-risk classification or low-risk recommendation.
rules.rationale: This operational rule prevents high-consequence label-supported or evidence-gap risk signals from being overridden by weaker synergy logic.
rules.evidence_directness: operational safety rule derived from CDS design

rules.name: Missing critical intake fields blocks low-risk classification
rules.provider_behavior: If required intake fields are missing, the AI must ask for missing data or output an incomplete-profile risk statement.
rules.rationale: Patient-factor rules depend on lab, medication, comorbidity and procedure data; absent data cannot be assumed normal.
rules.evidence_directness: operational data-quality rule

SRC_WEGOVY_DAILYMED_SEMAGLUTIDE.title: DailyMed label: WEGOVY (semaglutide)
SRC_WEGOVY_DAILYMED_SEMAGLUTIDE.directness: direct for semaglutide; class inference for other GLP-1-based or investigational agents
SRC_WEGOVY_DAILYMED_SEMAGLUTIDE.limitations: Does not establish risk magnitude for every investigational incretin/amylin/glucagon analogue or for all combinations.

SRC_MOUNJARO_DAILYMED_TIRZEPATIDE.title: DailyMed label: MOUNJARO (tirzepatide)
SRC_MOUNJARO_DAILYMED_TIRZEPATIDE.directness: direct for tirzepatide; pathway inference for GLP-1/GIP/glucagon polyagonists
SRC_MOUNJARO_DAILYMED_TIRZEPATIDE.limitations: Does not directly establish effects for retatrutide or other investigational polyagonists.

SRC_SYMLIN_DAILYMED_PRAMLINTIDE.title: DailyMed label: SYMLIN (pramlintide)
SRC_SYMLIN_DAILYMED_PRAMLINTIDE.directness: direct for pramlintide; pathway inference for amylin analogues such as cagrilintide
SRC_SYMLIN_DAILYMED_PRAMLINTIDE.limitations: Pramlintide data should not be over-applied to all amylin analogues without direct evidence.

SRC_BYETTA_DAILYMED_EXENATIDE.title: DailyMed label: BYETTA (exenatide)
SRC_BYETTA_DAILYMED_EXENATIDE.limitations: Does not apply automatically to all GLP-1 receptor agonists; semaglutide/tirzepatide have different renal pharmacology but can still carry dehydration/AKI warnings.

SRC_NOCDURNA_FDA_DESMOPRESSIN.title: FDA label: NOCDURNA (desmopressin acetate)
SRC_NOCDURNA_FDA_DESMOPRESSIN.directness: direct for desmopressin; class inference for vasopressin/ADH-axis agents
SRC_NOCDURNA_FDA_DESMOPRESSIN.limitations: Nocdurna label is indication-specific and notes it may not be the latest FDA label; cross-application to injectable/desmopressin formulations requires clinician review.

SRC_DESMOPRESSIN_PFIZER_INJECTION.title: Pfizer label: desmopressin acetate injection
SRC_DESMOPRESSIN_PFIZER_INJECTION.limitations: Does not cover every peptide with possible ADH-like biology.

SRC_EGRIFTA_FDA_TESAMORELIN.title: FDA label: EGRIFTA (tesamorelin)
SRC_EGRIFTA_FDA_TESAMORELIN.directness: direct for tesamorelin; pathway inference for GHRH/GHS/GH-axis research peptides
SRC_EGRIFTA_FDA_TESAMORELIN.limitations: Does not directly establish safety rules for CJC-1295, sermorelin, ipamorelin, GHRP-2/6, or hexarelin; those are lower-confidence pathway extrapolations.

SRC_HUMATROPE_DAILYMED_SOMATROPIN.title: DailyMed label: HUMATROPE (somatropin)
SRC_HUMATROPE_DAILYMED_SOMATROPIN.directness: direct for somatropin; indirect for secretagogues and GHRH analogues
SRC_HUMATROPE_DAILYMED_SOMATROPIN.limitations: Somatropin is exogenous GH, not the same as all GH secretagogues; extrapolation should be marked indirect.

SRC_TERIPARATIDE_DAILYMED.title: DailyMed label: teriparatide injection
SRC_TERIPARATIDE_DAILYMED.directness: direct for teriparatide; PTH/PTHrP class inference for abaloparatide
SRC_TERIPARATIDE_DAILYMED.limitations: Specific abaloparatide rules should be confirmed against its own label if used in production.

SRC_VYLEESI_FDA_BREMELANOTIDE.title: FDA label: VYLEESI (bremelanotide)
SRC_VYLEESI_FDA_BREMELANOTIDE.directness: direct for bremelanotide; indirect for PT-141 research framing where PT-141 refers to bremelanotide
SRC_VYLEESI_FDA_BREMELANOTIDE.limitations: Does not cover non-bremelanotide melanocortin analogues.

SRC_CAGRILINTIDE_SEMAGLUTIDE_PUBMED.title: Cagrilintide plus semaglutide human clinical studies (PubMed-indexed)
SRC_CAGRILINTIDE_SEMAGLUTIDE_PUBMED.directness: direct for cagrilintide/semaglutide combination; indirect for other amylin/incretin combinations
SRC_CAGRILINTIDE_SEMAGLUTIDE_PUBMED.limitations: Not a universal safety endorsement; studies have defined protocols, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and follow-up periods.

SRC_RETATRUTIDE_PUBMED.title: Retatrutide phase 2 obesity trial (PubMed)
SRC_RETATRUTIDE_PUBMED.directness: direct for retatrutide; indirect for other GLP-1/GIP/glucagon polyagonists
SRC_RETATRUTIDE_PUBMED.limitations: Phase 2 trial data do not establish broad patient-factor rules for all populations or combinations.

SRC_BPC157_REVIEW_PUBMED.title: BPC-157 review / translational evidence (PubMed-indexed)
SRC_BPC157_REVIEW_PUBMED.limitations: Does not support direct safety, efficacy, or combination claims in humans.

SRC_THYMOSIN_ALPHA1_REVIEW.title: Thymosin alpha-1 immunomodulatory review
SRC_THYMOSIN_ALPHA1_REVIEW.type: Review / human and mechanistic immunology literature
SRC_THYMOSIN_ALPHA1_REVIEW.limitations: Does not establish patient-specific safety rules for autoimmune disease, transplant, biologics, or all combinations.

SRC_LL37_REVIEW.title: LL-37/cathelicidin immunomodulatory and wound-healing review literature
SRC_LL37_REVIEW.limitations: Does not establish safety of exogenous LL-37 use or combinations in clinic.

SRC_GHK_CU_REVIEW.title: GHK-Cu wound-healing and skin-regeneration review literature
SRC_GHK_CU_REVIEW.type: Review / mechanistic and clinical dermatology literature
SRC_GHK_CU_REVIEW.directness: mechanistic/limited human topical or skin context
SRC_GHK_CU_REVIEW.limitations: Does not establish systemic peptide interaction rules or oncologic safety claims.

SRC_MOTSC_REVIEW.title: MOTS-c review: mitochondrial-derived peptide and metabolic biology
SRC_MOTSC_REVIEW.limitations: Does not support direct patient-factor safety rules in humans.

SRC_ELAMIPRETIDE_SS31_TRIAL.title: Elamipretide/SS-31 human trial safety literature
SRC_ELAMIPRETIDE_SS31_TRIAL.directness: direct for elamipretide in studied populations; not generalizable to all mitochondrial peptide use
SRC_ELAMIPRETIDE_SS31_TRIAL.limitations: Does not establish broad CDS rules for all comorbidities or combinations.

SRC_KISSPEPTIN_REVIEW.title: Kisspeptin/GnRH axis review literature
SRC_KISSPEPTIN_REVIEW.limitations: Does not establish safety or contraindications for every clinical context or combination.

SRC_NEUROPEPTIDE_RESEARCH_LIMITED.title: Selank/Semax neuropeptide research (PubMed-indexed); DSIP evidence-gap
SRC_NEUROPEPTIDE_RESEARCH_LIMITED.limitations: Search results and accessible snippets do not provide robust patient-factor safety rules; use only to support uncertainty language, not direct clinical claims.
